title: Grounds for Imposition of Administrative Sanctions Against an LMA.
content: 32 Commission by an LMA of any of the offenses below shall be a ground for the imposition 33 of the corresponding penalty. 34 35 I. SERIOUS OFFENSES. Serious offenses are punishable by cancellation of license 36 including applicable accessory penalties: 37 38 1. Deploying a seafarer who is below eighteen (18) years old or below the minimum 39 age requirement for overseas employment; 40 41 2. Engaging in acts of gross material misrepresentation for the purpose of securing 42 a license or renewal thereof, or transfer of accreditation of principal/employer 43 and/or enrollment of a ship, such as giving false information or documents; 44 3. Engaging in the recruitment or placement of seafarers for jobs harmful to public 45 health or morality or to the dignity of the Republic of the Philippines; 46 47 4. Engaging in acts of misrepresentation for the purpose of processing seafarers 48 through a crew order that pertains to: (1) non-existent work or vessel; (2) work or 49 position different from the actual overseas work; (3) position different from actual 50 work onboard; or (4) work with a different employer whether accredited or not with 51 the Department; 52 53 5. Transfer or change of ownership, directly or indirectly, of a single proprietorship 54 licensed to engage in overseas employment; 55 56 6. Charging or accepting directly or indirectly any amount of money, goods or 57 services, or any fee or bond for any purpose from an applicant seafarer; 58 59 7. Allowing a non-Filipino citizen to head or manage an LMA; 60 61 44 8. Passing on to the seafarer the fees and costs that are solely chargeable to the 1 principal/employer; 2 3 9. Becoming an officer or member of the Board of any corporation or partnership 4 engaged directly or indirectly in the management of a travel agency by any of the 5 Proponents; 6 7 10. Deploying seafarers whose employment and travel documents were not 8 processed by the Department; 9 10 11. Allowing a non-licensee or non-holder of authority to conduct recruitment and 11 placement activities on behalf of the agency; 12 13 12. Allowing a foreign national or entity to own more than 25% of the authorized capital 14 and/or participate in the management and/or operations of an LMA, except in the 15 role of a board director; 16 17 13. Unjustifiably refusing or unreasonably failing to act on any received request for 18 assistance, including request for repatriation, leading to death, severe 19 psychological impairment, sexual abuse, or maltreatment of the seafarer; 20 21 14. Committing acts of, as well as acts promoting Trafficking in Persons as defined by 22 R.A. No. 9208, as amended, and other relevant laws; 23 24 15. Conviction of a crime constituting graft and corrupt practices as defined by the 25 Revised Penal Code, R.A. Nos. 3019 and 6713, and other relevant laws, involving 26 any official, employee, or personnel of the Department; or 27 28 16. Failure to act on abandonment cases of seafarers, as defined under Part I, Rule 29 II, Item 1, herein. 30 31 II. LESS SERIOUS OFFENSES. Less serious offenses are punishable by suspension of 32 license from Six (6) Months and One (1) Day to One (1) Year plus accessory penalties, 33 or in lieu thereof, alternative penalties plus accessory penalties as may be imposed: 34 35 1. Obstructing or attempting to obstruct inspection by the Department or its duly 36 authorized representative/s; 37 38 2. Substituting or altering to the prejudice of the seafarer, employment contracts 39 approved by the Department, from the time of actual signing thereof by the parties 40 up to and including the period of expiration of the same, without the approval of the 41 Department; 42 43 3. Withholding or denying travel or other pertinent documents from an applicant 44 seafarer before departure for monetary or financial considerations, or for any other 45 reasons, other than those authorized under P.D. No. 442, as amended, and its 46 IRR; 47 48 4. Failure or refusal to present employment records, such as payroll and daily time 49 records, pay slips, or other documents, such as medical records, that belong or 50 pertain to the seafarer and that is in the possession of the LMA, when required by 51 the individual seafarer, the Secretary of the DMW, the Secretary of the DOLE, their 52 duly authorized representatives, or by the duly authorized representative of a 53 recognized organization provided that the maximum fine that may be imposed for 54 this offense shall not exceed Php 1,000,000.00, pursuant to R.A. No. 12021 and 55 its IRR; 56 57 5. Failure to furnish a seafarer with copies of all his/her pertinent medical reports 58 and/or medical records at no cost to the seafarer; 59 60 6. Engaging in recruitment activities in places other than that specified in the license 61 or branch authority without an SRA; 62 45 7. Appointing or designating agents, representatives, or employees without notice to 1 and acknowledgement from the Department within the period prescribed under the 2 Rules; 3 4 8. Withholding of seafarer’s salaries or remittances or allotments, SSS contributions, 5 and loan amortization or shortchanging/reduction thereof without justifiable 6 reasons; 7 8 9. Allowing persons who are otherwise disqualified from participating in the overseas 9 employment program under existing laws, rules and regulations to participate in 10 the management and operation of the agency; 11 12 10. Failure to reimburse necessary expenses incurred and paid by the seafarer in 13 connection with his documentation and processing for purposes of deployment, in 14 cases where an employment contract has been signed and deployment does not 15 take place without the seafarer's fault; 16 17 11. Failure to comply with any of the undertakings submitted to the Department; 18 19 12. Imposing a compulsory and exclusive arrangement whereby a seafarer is required 20 to undergo health examinations (PEME), training, seminar, instruction or schooling 21 of any kind only from specifically designated institutions, entities, or clinics, as the 22 case may be, unless the cost is shouldered by the principal/employer or LMA; 23 24 13. Imposing a compulsory and exclusive arrangement whereby a seafarer is required 25 to avail of a loan from a specifically designated institution, entity, or person; 26 27 14. Granting a loan to a seafarer with interest exceeding eight percent (8%) per annum 28 which will be used for payment of legal and allowable fees and making the seafarer 29 issue, either personally or through a guarantor or accommodation party, post-dated 30 checks in relation to the said loan; 31 32 15. Refusal to condone or renegotiate a loan incurred by the seafarer after the latter’s 33 employment contract has been prematurely terminated through no fault of the 34 seafarer; 35 36 16. Failure to submit reports on serious/critical incidents involving abandonment, 37 piracy, death, missing seafarer, terrorism, hijacking, armed robbery, harassments, 38 serious illness, and injury requiring repatriation; 39 40 17. Failure to comply with the required notice and reportorial requirement to the 41 seafarer's family or next of kin in case of critical and/or medical incidents under 42 R.A. No. 12021 and its IRR; 43 44 18. Falsifying or altering travel documents of an applicant seafarer in relation to 45 recruitment and deployment; 46 47 19. Failure to actually deploy a contracted seafarer within the validity of the OFW 48 Clearance without valid reason; 49 50 20. Engaging in any acts of misrepresentation in connection with recruitment and 51 placement of seafarers, such as furnishing or publishing any false notice, 52 information or document in relation to recruitment or employment; or 53 54 21. Violation of any or all of the provisions of R.A. No. 12021 and its IRR. 55 56 Habitual Commission of Less Serious Offenses. – An LMA which commits the same less 57 serious offense for five (5) times or more shall be considered a habitual offender. For this 58 purpose, the maximum of the penalty shall be imposed upon it. 59 60 46 III. LIGHT OFFENSES. Light offenses are punishable by Two (2) Months to Six (6) 1 Months suspension of license plus accessory penalties, or in lieu thereof, alternative 2 penalties plus accessory penalties as may be imposed: 3 4 1. Failure to submit other reports as required under R.A. No. 12021 and its IRR, these 5 Rules and other issuances of the Department, as well as other applicable laws, 6 rules and regulations; 7 8 2. Influencing any person or entity not to employ any seafarer who has not applied 9 for employment through the agency, or influencing any person or entity not to 10 employ any seafarer who has formed, joined, supported, contacted or is supported 11 by any union or workers’ organization; 12 13 3. Failure to report to the Department the termination or resignation of 14 representatives or employees within the prescribed period; 15 16 4. Failure to monitor and submit quarterly report on the status and condition of its 17 deployed seafarers; or 18 19 5. Violations of other pertinent provisions of these Rules and other relevant laws, 20 rules and regulations, guidelines and other issuances on recruitment and 21 placement of seafarers for overseas employment and the protection of their 22 welfare. 23 24
